lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਅਗੈ ਕੁਸਟੀ ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਫਿਟਕੇ ਜਿ ਓਸੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਕੁਸਟੁ ਉਠਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: oe agai kusattee gur ke fittake ji os milai tis kusatt utthaahee |
lines.english: They have already become like lepers; cursed by the Guru, whoever meets them is also afflicted with leprosy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਤਿਨ ਕਾ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਨਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਜੋ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: har tin kaa darasan naa karahu jo doojai bhaae chit laahee |
lines.english: O Lord, I pray that I may not even catch sight of those, who focus their consciousness on the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਰਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਆਪਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਤਿਸੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਕਿਹੁ ਚਾਰਾ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: dhur karatai aap likh paaeaa tis naal kihu chaaraa naahee |
lines.english: That which the Creator pre-ordained from the very beginning - there can be no escape from that.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਵੈ ਕੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਵਡੀ ਹੈ ਨਿਤ ਸਵਾਈ ਚੜੈ ਚੜਾਹੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naavai kee vaddiaaee vaddee hai nit savaaee charrai charraahee |2|
lines.english: Great is the greatness of His Name; it increases, day by day. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਰਾਧਿ ਤੂ ਤਿਸੁ ਅਪੜਿ ਕੋ ਨ ਸਕਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jan naanak naam araadh too tis aparr ko na sakaahee |
lines.english: O servant Nanak, worship and adore the Naam, the Name of the Lord; no one can equal it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿ ਹੋਂਦੈ ਗੁਰੂ ਬਹਿ ਟਿਕਿਆ ਤਿਸੁ ਜਨ ਕੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਵਡੀ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: ji hondai guroo beh ttikiaa tis jan kee vaddiaaee vaddee hoee |
lines.english: Great is the greatness of that humble being, whom the Guru Himself anointed in His Presence.

lines.translit: mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਉ ਖੰਡ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਨਮਸਕਾਰੁ ਕਰਹਿ ਜਿਸ ਕੈ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਹਥੁ ਧਰਿਆ ਗੁਰਿ ਪੂਰੈ ਸੋ ਪੂਰਾ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau khandd brahamandd namasakaar kareh jis kai masatak hath dhariaa gur poorai so pooraa hoee |
lines.english: The galaxies and solar systems bow in reverence to him; the Perfect Guru has placed His hand upon his head, and he has become perfect.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਕਉ ਜਗਤੁ ਨਿਵਿਆ ਸਭੁ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਇਆ ਜਸੁ ਵਰਤਿਆ ਲੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: tis kau jagat niviaa sabh pairee peaa jas varatiaa loee |
lines.english: All the world comes and bows to him, falling at his feet. His praises spread throughout the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਨਿਤ ਚੜੈ ਸਵਾਈ ਅਪੜਿ ਕੋ ਨ ਸਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee vaddiaaee nit charrai savaaee aparr ko na sakoee |
lines.english: The glorious greatness of the Guru increases day by day; no one can equal it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਆਪਿ ਬਹਿ ਟਿਕਿਆ ਆਪੇ ਪੈਜ ਰਖੈ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jan naanak har karatai aap beh ttikiaa aape paij rakhai prabh soee |3|
lines.english: O servant Nanak, the Creator Lord Himself established him; God preserves his honor. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਉਦਾ ਜੋ ਕਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਸਮਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh saudaa jo kare har vasat samaale |
lines.english: The Gurmukh who comes to trade gathers the cargo of the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਵਣਜੀਐ ਹੀਰੇ ਪਰਵਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: naam nidhaan har vanajeeai heere paravaale |
lines.english: He deals in the treasure of the Lord's Name, the jewels and the diamonds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਕੋਟੁ ਅਪਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਹਟਨਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa kott apaar hai andar hattanaale |
lines.english: The human body is a great fortress, with its shops and streets within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਕਾਇਆ ਜਿ ਹੋਰ ਥੈ ਧਨੁ ਖੋਜਦੇ ਸੇ ਮੂੜ ਬੇਤਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: vin kaaeaa ji hor thai dhan khojade se moorr betaale |
lines.english: Those who search for this treasure outside of the body, in other places, are foolish demons.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਉਝੜਿ ਭਰਮਿ ਭਵਾਈਅਹਿ ਜਿਉ ਝਾੜ ਮਿਰਗੁ ਭਾਲੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: se ujharr bharam bhavaaeeeh jiau jhaarr mirag bhaale |15|
lines.english: They wander around in the wilderness of doubt, like the deer who searches for the musk in the bushes. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕਰੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਕੀ ਸੁ ਅਉਖਾ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਹੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jo nindaa kare satigur poore kee su aaukhaa jag meh hoeaa |
lines.english: One who slanders the Perfect True Guru, shall have difficulty in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Salok, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰਕ ਘੋਰੁ ਦੁਖ ਖੂਹੁ ਹੈ ਓਥੈ ਪਕੜਿ ਓਹੁ ਢੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: narak ghor dukh khoohu hai othai pakarr ohu dtoeaa |
lines.english: He is caught and thrown into the most horrible hell, the well of pain and suffering.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂਕ ਪੁਕਾਰ ਕੋ ਨ ਸੁਣੇ ਓਹੁ ਅਉਖਾ ਹੋਇ ਹੋਇ ਰੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kook pukaar ko na sune ohu aaukhaa hoe hoe roeaa |
lines.english: No one listens to his shrieks and cries; he cries out in pain and misery.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਹੁ ਤੇਲੀ ਸੰਦਾ ਬਲਦੁ ਕਰਿ ਨਿਤ ਭਲਕੇ ਉਠਿ ਪ੍ਰਭਿ ਜੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: ohu telee sandaa balad kar nit bhalake utth prabh joeaa |
lines.english: He is like the ox at the oil-press; each morning when he rises, God places the yoke upon him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਿ ਹਲਤੁ ਪਲਤੁ ਸਭੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਲਾਹਾ ਮੂਲੁ ਸਭੁ ਖੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: on halat palat sabh gavaaeaa laahaa mool sabh khoeaa |
lines.english: He totally loses this world and the next; he has lost all of his investment and profit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਵੇਖੈ ਸੁਣੈ ਨਿਤ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਤਿਦੂ ਕਿਛੁ ਗੁਝਾ ਨ ਹੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: har vekhai sunai nit sabh kichh tidoo kichh gujhaa na hoeaa |
lines.english: The Lord always sees and hears everything; nothing can be concealed from Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਆਪਣੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਣ ਧੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jis kripaa kare prabh aapanee tis satigur ke charan dhoeaa |
lines.english: One who is blessed by God's Grace washes the feet of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸਾ ਬੀਜੇ ਸੋ ਲੁਣੈ ਜੇਹਾ ਪੁਰਬਿ ਕਿਨੈ ਬੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jaisaa beeje so lunai jehaa purab kinai boeaa |
lines.english: As you plant, so shall you harvest, according to what you planted in the past.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇ ਤੂ ਜਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jan naanak naam dhiaae too jap har har naam sukh hoeaa |1|
lines.english: O servant Nanak, meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord; chanting the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, peace is obtained. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪਿਛੈ ਤਰਿ ਗਇਆ ਜਿਉ ਲੋਹਾ ਕਾਠ ਸੰਗੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur satigur pichhai tar geaa jiau lohaa kaatth sangoeaa |
lines.english: He is carried across by the Guru, the True Guru, like iron which is carried across by wood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਭਾਗੀਆ ਸੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਜਿਨਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vaddabhaageea sohaaganee jinaa guramukh miliaa har raae |
lines.english: Very fortunate is the soul-bride, who, as Gurmukh, meets the Lord, her King.

lines.translit: mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਸਭੁ ਧਰਮੁ ਹੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਚੇ ਕੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਜੋਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: eihu sareer sabh dharam hai jis andar sache kee vich jot |
lines.english: This body is the home of Dharma; the Divine Light of the True Lord is within it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰ ਜੋਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੀਆ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: antar jot pragaaseea naanak naam samaae |2|
lines.english: Her inner being is illiminated with His Divine Light; O Nanak, she is absorbed in His Name. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਆਤਮ ਰਾਮੁ ਪਛਾਣਿਆ ਤਾਂ ਇਕੁ ਰਵਿਆ ਇਕੋ ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: sabh aatam raam pachhaaniaa taan ik raviaa iko ot pot |
lines.english: When someone realizes the All-pervading Soul, then he sees the One and Only Lord permeating, through and through.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਹਜ ਰਤਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਲੁਕਿ ਰਹੇ ਕੋਈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਕਢੈ ਖੋਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: guhaj ratan vich luk rahe koee guramukh sevak kadtai khot |
lines.english: Hidden within it are the jewels of mystery; how rare is that Gurmukh, that selfless servant, who digs them out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਇਕੁ ਮੰਨਿਆ ਇਕੋ ਸੁਣਿਆ ਸ੍ਰਵਣ ਸਰੋਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik dekhiaa ik maniaa iko suniaa sravan sarot |
lines.english: He sees the One, he believes in the One, and with his ears, he listens only to the One.
